# WebArea

A service that lets you host your website serverless via a link that encodes the whole site.

**No absolute paths supported** right now as i am using the subpath /WebArea/ in [hasselassel.github.io/WebArea/](https://hasselassel.github.io/WebArea/) and an absolute path would overwrite this subpath.

Absolute paths will hence obviously be supported when i get a "real" domain (money) or update the service worker (boring string management).

**No folder strucutre supported** either right now, as i dont think you can upload a folder (no research done, but assuming). Easily solvable with zip; But who cares, just flatten everything.

Rather use extern image like resources as they don't compress well and will make the link huge (maybe even too huge).

The default file is index.html. It also needs like a second to build.

Also I dont know css so not pretty.

Obviously inspired by [TextArea](https://textarea.my/#80jNyclXSCvKz1UIT01yLEpNVPgwf8lkAA==)

#### [Link to Website](https://hasselassel.github.io/WebArea/)

## Examples

[Self Reference to WebArea 🤯](http://localhost:8000/#) (or some older version if I update)
